title: JavaScript Runtime, Tooling, and Evidence · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Trace a small service-status script from source through parsing, execution, console evidence, and a user-visible result. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a reproducible service-status runtime notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish ECMAScript language behavior from browser, worker, and server host APIs.
Trace source parsing, module instantiation, execution contexts, jobs, and observable output.
Use console evidence, breakpoints, scope inspection, watch expressions, and stack traces without relying on logging alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Runtime Host evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Runtime Host assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Normalize service inputs [" 4 ", 4, null, {value:4}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Runtime Host has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Runtime Host precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Normalize service inputs [" 4 ", 4, null, {value:4}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: JavaScript and the browser are the same runtime specification.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested

Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling

stimulus.title: Reproducible Environment case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Normalize service inputs [" 6 ", 6, null, {value:6}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record runtime, module mode, inputs, locale, time, and dependency conditions needed to reproduce behavior. Artifact under review: a reproducible service-status runtime notebook.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

content.title: Evidence defense
